title: Effects of Different Types of Virtual Reality Display on Presence and Learning in a Safety Training Scenario
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
category: C. Immersion, presence and display fidelity
evidence_type: Knowledge gain; self-efficacy; engagement; presence; two-week follow-up
technology_level: Desktop VR; narrow-FOV HMD; wide-FOV HMD
short_summary: Compares desktop VR and two HMD conditions in a procedural safety serious game, showing that display type affects presence and engagement more clearly than retained knowledge or self-efficacy.

title: Development of fire safety behavioral skills via virtual reality
category: B. Effectiveness of VR fire safety training
method: Design-based research; VR-based behavioral skills training
application_domain: Fire safety behavioral skills for children
evidence_type: Ten primary school students; behavioral observation; transfer to real environment
source_path: Sun ConnectedPapers direct / related fire safety VR literature
short_summary: Designs a VR-based behavioral skills training environment and reports improved fire safety skills, with most students able to transfer learned behaviors to real environments.

title: A review of immersive virtual reality serious games to enhance learning and training
application_domain: Immersive VR serious games for learning and training
evidence_type: Review of 135 immersive VR serious game proposals with end-user validation
short_summary: Reviews immersive VR serious games and analyzes application domains, audiences, game design, technological implementation, and evaluation practices.

authors: Feng, Z.; González, V. A.; Amor, R.; Lovreglio, R.; Cabrera-Guerrero, G.
title: Immersive virtual reality serious games for evacuation training and research: A systematic literature review
evidence_type: Conceptual framework; reviewed IVR serious game studies
short_summary: Reviews IVR serious games for evacuation training and proposes a framework linking pedagogy, game environment development, outcomes, and user experience.

authors: Gagliardi, E.; Bernardini, G.; Quagliarini, E.; Schumacher, M.; Calvaresi, D.
title: Characterization and future perspectives of Virtual Reality Evacuation Drills for safe built environments: A Systematic Literature Review
application_domain: VR/IVR evacuation drills; safe built environments
short_summary: Reviews VR/IVR evacuation drills and identifies limits in realism, user inclusiveness, NPC modelling, multisensory effects, and emergency-system formalization.

authors: Lawson, G.; Roper, T.; Shaw, E.; Hsieh, M. K.; Cobb, S. V.
title: Multimodal virtual environments: an opportunity to improve fire safety training?
venue: Policy and Practice in Health and Safety
short_summary: Argues that visual, audio, olfactory, and thermal cues can improve realism and behavioural validity in virtual fire safety training.

authors: Lovreglio, R.; Duan, X.; Rahouti, A.; Phipps, R.; Nilsson, D.
title: Comparing the effectiveness of fire extinguisher virtual reality and video training
category: B. Effectiveness of VR fire safety training
short_summary: Compares VR and video fire extinguisher training and finds stronger knowledge acquisition and self-efficacy outcomes for VR.

authors: Monteiro, P.; Melo, M.; Valente, A.; Vasconcelos-Raposo, J.; Bessa, M.
title: Delivering Critical Stimuli for Decision Making in VR Training: Evaluation Study of a Firefighter Training Scenario
evidence_type: 48 subjects; performance; knowledge transfer; presence; cybersickness; usability
technology_level: Immersive VR with thermal haptic device
short_summary: Evaluates thermal cues in a firefighter VR scenario and shows that task-relevant stimuli are essential for realistic decision-making performance.

authors: Morélot, S.; Garrigou, A.; Dedieu, J.; N’Kaoua, B.
title: Virtual reality for fire safety training: Influence of immersion and sense of presence on conceptual and procedural acquisition
category: C. Immersion, presence and display fidelity
evidence_type: Conceptual/procedural acquisition; presence measures; user characteristics
technology_level: Text; PC VR; motion-based immersive VR
short_summary: Shows that immersion supports procedural learning but not conceptual learning, and that presence did not significantly affect either outcome in the studied task.

authors: Rahouti, A.; Lovreglio, R.; Datoussaïd, S.; Descamps, T.
title: Prototyping and validating a non-immersive virtual reality serious game for healthcare fire safety training
category: B. Effectiveness of VR fire safety training
evidence_type: 78 staff; knowledge acquisition; retention; self-efficacy; motivation
short_summary: Develops and validates a non-immersive VR serious game for hospital fire safety training, comparing it against slide-based lectures.

authors: Ristor, R.; Morélot, S.; Garrigou, A.; N’Kaoua, B.
title: Virtual reality for fire safety training: Study of factors involved in immersive learning
category: C. Immersion, presence and display fidelity
evidence_type: Conceptual/procedural learning; motivation; cognitive load; emotion; presence
short_summary: Studies how immersion, presence, motivation, cognitive load, and emotions influence conceptual and procedural learning in VR fire safety training.

authors: Saghafian, M.; Laumann, K.; Akhtar, R. S.; Skogstad, M. R.
title: The Evaluation of Virtual Reality Fire Extinguisher Training
category: B. Effectiveness of VR fire safety training
evidence_type: 85 participants; questionnaire; observation notes; thematic analysis
short_summary: Evaluates trainees’ perceptions of VR fire extinguisher training and identifies realism, emotional/bodily experience, repeatability, and safety as major themes.

authors: Scorgie, D.; Feng, Z.; Paes, D.; Parisi, F.; Yiu, T. W.; Lovreglio, R.
title: Virtual reality for safety training: A systematic literature review and meta-analysis
application_domain: Safety training; fire; construction; aviation; mining
short_summary: Reviews VR safety training across domains and provides meta-analytic evidence that VR can outperform traditional training in knowledge acquisition and retention.

authors: Sun, L.; Lee, B. G.; Chung, W.-Y.
title: Enhancing Fire Safety Education Through Immersive Virtual Reality Training with Serious Gaming and Haptic Feedback
technology_level: High-immersive VR; low-immersive VR; non-immersive baseline
short_summary: Introduces FEET and compares high-immersive VR, low-immersive VR, and a non-immersive baseline to evaluate learning, user experience, and interaction preferences.
